artist: Attributed to Reb Azriel David Fastag
overview: While the text is based on Maimonides' 12th Principle of Faith, this specific version became the "Hymn of the Camps" during the Holocaust. Tradition holds that Reb Azriel David Fastag, a Modzitzer Hasid, composed this haunting melody in a cattle car bound for Treblinka in 1942. He wrote it to sustain the spirits of his fellow Jews, asserting that even in the absolute darkness of the Shoah, faith in the eventual redemption remained unshakable. It is now a global symbol of Jewish resilience.
poem_lines: in the coming of the Messiah;
nonetheless, I will wait for him
every single day that he may come.
